items.title: Update Tencent Cloud SDK to Latest Version in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the `tencentcloud-sdk-python` package from version 3.1.75 to 3.1.80, enhancing the functionality and performance of the SDK. The changes include various improvements and bug fixes as outlined in the release notes. This update ensures that the application remains compatible with the latest features provided by Tencent Cloud.

items.title: Update workflow to use OpenAI coding agent in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the workflow to replace the deprecated `copilot-swe-agent` with `openai-code-agent`, ensuring that ruleset violations are surfaced instead of causing silent failures. The change enhances visibility for research issues by allowing comments on violations to be displayed. Additionally, repo admins will need to manually add `openai-code-agent` to the bypass list for smooth operation.

items.title: Reverted to Copilot Agent for Workflow Success in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the assignment of the coding agent back to the legacy Copilot agent in the workflow. The change addresses issues with the newer agents, which encountered repository ruleset violations. By restoring the previous setup, we aim to enhance workflow stability and ensure successful issue handling.

items.title: Refactor for TypeScript and Python Monorepo in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update splits the repository into a monorepo structure, introducing a new Python package alongside the existing TypeScript package. The Python package is designed for feature parity with the TypeScript version, enabling seamless integration with the AceDataCloud SDK. Key enhancements include CI workflows for both languages and a robust Python payment handler for X402 transactions.

items.title: Route Research to OpenAI Coding Agent in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the workflow to route research tasks to the OpenAI coding agent instead of the Claude bot, which was blocked by a repository ruleset violation. The changes include updating the suggested actors filter and the step name in the scheduled research workflow. This adjustment ensures smoother operation and continued progress on the project.

items.title: Streamline SDK Integration by Removing Legacy Client in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the redundant `createX402Client` and related functions from the codebase, streamlining the integration with the `@acedatacloud/sdk`. Key changes include the deletion of `src/client.ts`, updates to the README for clarity, and the removal of outdated types. This cleanup ensures that all application paths utilize the SDK, enhancing maintainability and reducing complexity.

items.title: Enhance Bot Assignment with PAT in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the workflow for bot assignment by utilizing a user-level Personal Access Token (PAT) instead of the workflow token, which previously returned a FORBIDDEN error. The change ensures that the bot can be assigned correctly, improving functionality for the Claude agent. Additionally, it maintains compatibility with forks by falling back to the workflow token when necessary.

items.title: Enhance Payment Handling with New Adapter in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `createX402PaymentHandler`, a new adapter that integrates seamlessly with the `@acedatacloud/sdk`. It simplifies the payment process by allowing the X402 Client to act as a plugin for the SDK, enhancing user experience without the need for a separate fetch wrapper. The README has been updated to reflect this integration, guiding users towards the SDK for production use.

items.title: Introducing Pluggable Payment Handler for Enhanced X402 Integration in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a pluggable `paymentHandler` hook for handling `402 Payment Required` responses in the SDK. It allows the SDK to seamlessly integrate with on-chain payment solutions, eliminating the need for separate HTTP clients. Key changes include new TypeScript and Python implementations, along with updated documentation to guide users through the new integration pattern.

items.title: Update assignment workflow to use Claude agent in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the Copilot coding agent with the anthropic-code-agent in the assignment workflow. The change involves switching from a REST API to GraphQL to ensure proper handling of bot logins. This enhancement improves the reliability of issue assignments in the workflow.

items.title: Streamline GitHub Token Usage in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the workflow to utilize the default GITHUB_TOKEN for assigning the Copilot bot, resolving the 403 error encountered with the SOLRADAR_GITHUB_TOKEN. Key changes include pinning the create-issue and assign-copilot steps to the GITHUB_TOKEN, ensuring proper permissions and functionality.

items.title: Enhance Autonomous Agent Capabilities in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms SolRadar into a fully autonomous agent-operated research and trading system. Key additions include comprehensive documentation in `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`, detailing mission objectives and operational guidelines. New skills for narrative signals and position management have been introduced, enhancing the agent's decision-making capabilities while maintaining strict budget controls.

items.title: Fix bot login for auto-merge guard in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the bot login from `AceDataCloudDev` to `acedatacloud-dev` in the auto-merge guard configuration. This change ensures that reports pushed by the development bot are accepted, preventing unnecessary rejections when Copilot is disabled. The modification enhances the reliability of the auto-merge process.

items.title: Strengthen Auto-Merge Guards for Reports in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces stricter auto-merge policies for pull requests affecting the reports directory. Now, a PR will only be auto-merged if it meets all four specified criteria, including author verification and file type restrictions. This change enhances security by preventing unauthorized or inappropriate changes from being merged automatically.

items.title: New buyback transaction added in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new buyback transaction recorded on April 19, 2026, where $59 USDC was converted into 168,000 ACE tokens. The transaction details, including the source and venue, have been added to the buyback history for transparency. This enhancement supports better tracking of on-chain buyback activities.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Merge for Reports in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a 5-minute cron job to the auto-merge workflow for reports-only pull requests. It addresses the issue where PRs created by GitHub Apps, like the copilot-swe-agent, were not triggering the necessary workflows for auto-merging. The new triggers ensure that these PRs are efficiently processed without manual intervention, maintaining safety checks for file types.

items.title: Automated research cycle completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The automated research cycle for April 19, 2026, has been completed, but all external data providers were unreachable, leading to a graceful degradation. Nine watchlist mints were reviewed, and all were skipped due to low liquidity and lack of security data. Source errors were logged for DEX Screener and GitHub, while Birdeye was not attempted due to a missing API key.

items.title: Revamped README with Flow Diagrams and Real Transactions in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: The README has been completely rewritten to address key user questions, including the payment flow and on-chain proofs. It now features a detailed sequence diagram, a setup guide, and a proof table with clickable transaction hashes from various chains. This update enhances user understanding and accessibility without any code changes.

items.title: Enhanced Billing Regression Testing for X402 in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a parameterized end-to-end test script that validates dynamic pricing and API usage recording for multiple APIs in the X402 billing system. Key scenarios include various models and their expected credits, ensuring accurate billing and API response handling. The script has been tested successfully, confirming that all pricing matches expected values and API usage records are created as intended.

items.title: Streamline HTTP Logic by Dropping Duplicates in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update removes five redundant shell scripts that were merely wrapping HTTP calls already handled by the `solradar_cli`. The new `analyze_watchlist.sh` script has been added to streamline the process of analyzing tokens from a watchlist without duplicating HTTP logic. Additionally, updates to the SKILL.md files clarify the usage of the CLI and ensure that all network calls are centralized.

items.title: Revamped Skills to Meet Agent Skills SPEC in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update restructures the skills directory to comply with the Agent Skills SPEC, enhancing the Copilot coding agent's ability to discover and utilize skills effectively. Key changes include the addition of YAML front matter, improved file organization, and new scripts for executing tasks. The workflow has also been tweaked to streamline the research cycle process, ensuring a more efficient operation.

items.title: Integrate SDK, SolRadar, and X402Client as Submodules in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update registers the `SDK`, `SolRadar`, and `X402Client` as git submodules, tracking the `main` branch. The configuration follows the existing project convention with `ignore = all`. These additions enhance modularity and maintainability within the project.

items.title: Enhance billing rules for aichat2 conversations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a cost file for the aichat2 API, ensuring accurate billing for chat completions. The new JSON file references shared pricing models, aligning with the legacy aichat API. This change will enable the PlatformGateway to log and deduct costs appropriately during usage.

items.title: Enhance LLM Usage Reporting for Accurate Billing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new utility to report LLM usage back to the PlatformGateway, ensuring accurate billing for aichat2 conversations. The implementation mirrors legacy behavior while adding robust error handling and honoring usage exemptions. Key changes include modifications to the conversations handler and the addition of a new recording utility to streamline the billing process.

items.title: Transforming SolRadar into an Agent-Operated Research Loop in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update pivots SolRadar's workflow to focus on agent-operated research, allowing Copilot to run research cycles and publish reports directly. Key changes include the introduction of a new `skills/` directory, modifications to the `Scheduled Research` workflow, and the implementation of an auto-merge process for report PRs. This shift enhances the efficiency of report generation while maintaining strict guidelines for automated processes.

items.title: Automate npm Publishing with CalVer in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update revamps the npm publish pipeline to automatically release new versions of `@acedatacloud/x402-client` on every push to the main branch. Utilizing a date-based CalVer scheme, it ensures unique versioning throughout the day without manual intervention. The workflow now includes multiple jobs for building, publishing, and creating GitHub releases, streamlining the deployment process significantly.

items.title: Exciting New Chat Models Added in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new ChatGPT models: gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, and gpt-5.4-nano. Key changes include the addition of new constants in the chat model group and updates to the internationalization files for English and Chinese. The modifications enhance the model offerings and improve user experience across supported languages.

items.title: Automate CalVer Versioning for NPM Releases in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the static semver versioning with a CalVer format (`YYYY.M.D`) for npm publishes, aligning with AceDataCloud's MCP server standards. A new script automatically updates the `package.json` version at publish time, ensuring accurate release dates without manual intervention. The README has been updated to document this new release process.

items.title: Enhance AI Skills Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the AI Skills dialog in Nexior by ensuring skills load on the first open and introducing functionality for creating and deleting custom skills. Users can now add skills through a new interface that supports various skill types and file uploads, along with a delete option for user-owned skills. Additionally, English and zh-CN localization strings have been added to support these features.

items.title: Temporarily Hide Desktop Agent in Nexior 🛠️
items.summary: This update hides the Desktop Agent toolbar entry in the Nexior chat conversation by using a conditional rendering approach. The underlying `DesktopAgentManager` component and its state remain intact, allowing for easy re-enabling in the future. This change aims to streamline the user interface while maintaining functionality.

items.title: Enhance Sidebar Logo and Padding in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the sidebar by enlarging the brand logo and balancing the padding. The logo height has been increased from 44px to 52px, and the padding has been adjusted to 6px top and 4px bottom. These changes ensure that the logo visually aligns with the service icons below, enhancing the overall user interface.

items.title: Enhance E2E Testing with Base and Solana Scripts in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Base and Solana end-to-end (E2E) testing scripts, which are now available for broader use. Both scripts securely read payer private keys from environment variables, ensuring no secrets are committed. The Solana RPC defaults to the public mainnet-beta endpoint, enhancing flexibility for testing environments.

items.title: Enhance detection and scoring for low-quality batches in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new properties to the Candidate model for better detection of 'pump.fun' launches and social presence. A scoring penalty is applied for candidates lacking social links, and a new Batch Analysis section has been added to research reports to highlight these findings. These changes aim to improve the quality of analyzed batches and provide clearer insights into candidate performance.

items.title: Enhancing Provider Reliability and Scoring Heuristics in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update focuses on analyzing the research cycle output to identify areas for improvement in provider reliability. Key changes include the addition of duplicate-symbol detection in the pipeline and a scoring penalty for duplicates. Enhancements to the automation cycle report and comprehensive testing are also planned to ensure robustness.

items.title: Enhance chat submission readiness in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `ready` prop to the Composer component, which disables the submit button until the application and credentials are fully initialized. This change prevents premature message submissions that could lead to errors during the chat initialization process. The fix has been verified and is part of ongoing improvements following the aichat2 redesign.

items.title: Streamline PRs with Copilot Auto-Merge in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automated workflow for merging pull requests authored by the Copilot agent or those in the copilot/* branches. The new configuration ensures that PRs are automatically merged once they are ready for review and have passed CI checks, enhancing the efficiency of the development process.

items.title: Enhance provider resilience with graceful error handling in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the error handling for Birdeye, DexScreener, and GitHub clients, preventing full enrichment failures during provider outages. Key changes include returning empty results instead of crashing and adding a new counter for candidates with provider warnings in the automation report. Comprehensive tests have also been added to ensure graceful degradation of service during network errors.

items.title: Enhance file attachment handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of non-image file attachments by converting them into upstream-safe text blocks before dispatching to the LLM. Image blocks remain unchanged, ensuring that existing tool_result splitting is preserved. Additionally, new tests have been added to cover the file attachment message conversion, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance SSE Error Handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of Server-Sent Events (SSE) errors in the chat feature. It forwards SSE error messages through the chat operator response and ensures that the streaming conversation promise is rejected when an error event is emitted by the backend. This change prevents blank assistant responses by rendering visible error messages instead.

items.title: Enhance npm Publishing Workflow in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust GitHub Actions CI workflow for npm operations, including installation, building, and packaging. It also adds a publish workflow triggered by GitHub Releases, ensuring safer scoped-package publishing with updated configurations. The README has been enhanced with current npm status, release flow, and verification results for improved clarity.

items.title: Enhance Payment Regression Script in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive regression script for the `/openai/chat/completions` endpoint, covering payment flows for base, Solana, and Skale networks in a single run. Key improvements include switching to the real SDK flow for Solana transactions and enhancing CLS matching robustness. The script is designed to work seamlessly with existing internal tools and secrets.

items.title: Sync to Nexior version 3.34.0 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the repository's release state with the already-published `@acedatacloud/nexior@3.34.0`. It includes the removal of consumed change files and records the changelog entry for version 3.34.0. Additionally, the `publish` workflow on `main` has been unblocked, resolving issues with repeated republishing attempts.

items.title: Enhance Local Execution and Artifact Flow in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces local execution capabilities for the `code_execute` tool, prioritizing the connected desktop agent with a fallback to the cloud sandbox. Additionally, the `file_manager` has been extended to support local file operations and artifact uploads, enhancing the multimodal conversation experience. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the reliability of the new features.

items.title: Enhance Artifact Messaging in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update extends the chat functionality to include artifact metadata and structured message payloads. It allows for the rendering of artifact events in the conversation stream as image or file blocks, improving the message preview UI by displaying file names instead of URLs. These changes enhance user experience by providing clearer context for shared files.

items.title: Update Nanobanana Upstream to Mountsea in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: The default `NANOBANANA_BASE_URL` has been switched from the `openaihk` supplier to `mountsea` to improve compatibility with the `nano-banana-2` model. This change ensures that requests to `/v1/images/generations` and `/v1/images/edits` are correctly routed without encountering bad requests. The worker code remains unchanged, requiring only a redeployment to apply the new settings.

items.title: Enhanced Debug Logging for X402 Facilitator in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces trace-id aware logging for verify and settle requests in the X402 facilitator. Key request details such as network, nonce, payTo, asset, amount, and payer context are now logged, enhancing failure and replay diagnostics while maintaining existing settlement behavior. The changes aim to improve debugging and monitoring capabilities.

items.title: Enhanced Debug Logging and Metadata Fixes in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the SKALE EIP-712 domain metadata to align with the on-chain USDC.e contract. Additionally, it introduces x402 request/response summaries and trace-id forwarding for facilitator verify/settle calls, while preserving richer diagnostics for x402 records, including missing settlement context and failed transaction hashes.

items.title: Enhance Error Recovery Modules for Aichat2 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces dedicated error recovery modules for handling prompt length issues, output token limits, and fallback models in Aichat2. Key changes include the implementation of retry mechanisms in the query loop and the addition of integration tests for all recovery paths, ensuring a more robust user experience. These enhancements aim to improve system resilience and performance during error scenarios.

items.title: Enhance aichat2 Context Pipeline for Better Performance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated truncation layer for context fitting, ensuring that auto-compact summaries are preserved during hard truncation. Additionally, focused tests have been added to validate tool result budgeting, auto-compaction, truncation, and fallback mechanisms in the query loop. These changes aim to improve the efficiency and reliability of the aichat2 context management.

items.title: Revamp aichat2 Tool Orchestration for Enhanced Performance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the monolithic `AgenticEngine` with a more modular `QueryEngine` and `queryLoop`, allowing for improved tool orchestration. Key changes include the introduction of concurrency-safe tools that can run in parallel, while write tools remain serialized. Additionally, the update enhances conversation persistence and introduces focused tests for better validation.

items.title: Enhance X402 Client Protocol Handling in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the EVM envelopes with the new `x402Version: 2` and makes the Solana client ESM-safe by replacing lazy `require(...)` with dynamic imports. Additionally, the README now documents the verified flows for `base`, `solana`, and `skale`, ensuring that users have accurate integration guidance. The changes improve compatibility and functionality for browser and ESM consumers.

items.title: Enhance x402 Handling in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `/record` endpoint to read the `payflow` value from the request query string as a fallback. This ensures that x402 requests are processed correctly, allowing them to enter the dedicated settlement branch without being prematurely blocked by the application ID check. As a result, requests from Base, Solana, and SKALE will now be handled reliably.

items.title: Archived AI Orchestrator Design Document in Index 📁
items.summary: This update successfully archives the AI Orchestrator design document into the .plans/ directory. The document, previously located in DESIGN-AI-ORCHESTRATOR.md, has been added with a total of 4916 lines. This change helps streamline project documentation and organization.

items.title: Streamline GEO Plans and Normalize AGENTS Guide in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the GEO growth plan into a single canonical document while removing outdated entries from the backlog. The root assistant guide has been renamed to AGENTS.md for consistency, ensuring that tools can reliably access project instructions. The changes enhance clarity in planning and documentation, improving overall project organization.

items.title: Enhance MCP OAuth Callback Handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the MCP OAuth popup callback flow in the chat UI by allowing successful completion with only a `code` parameter. It also ensures that the `state` parameter is forwarded when present, maintaining compatibility with existing connector OAuth flows. Additionally, the authorization button now properly resets when the popup is closed, enhancing user experience.
